C002.result_snippet: In physics, coherence expresses the potential for two waves to interfere. Two monochromatic beams from a single source always interfere.: 286 Even for wave sources that are not strictly monochromatic, they may still be partly coherent. Without coherence, interference fringes fade and no stable pattern emerges.These quantities are interdependent — coherence allows meaningful phase and path comparisons, and interference emerges as their observable consequence.
